name: Inbound Email with Suspicious Subject Keywords
notes:
This rule triggers when an email is received with a suspicious subject or a subject conveying some sense of urgency. 

Note: Update the regular expression to include suspicious keywords.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Mail
when the event category for the event is one of the following Application.Mail Receive
when an event matches any of the following Inbound Email with Suspicious Subject
when the event matches Subject (custom) is not N/A
when any of Subject (custom) match (?i)congratulations|\burgent|pharmacy|lottery|payment|purchase|deposit|refund|voice\s*mail|charity|donation|delivery|tax|heritage
TD_list: APPLY Inbound Email with Suspicious Subject Keywords on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Mail
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following Application.Mail Receive
AND NOT when an event matches any of the following Inbound Email with Suspicious Subject
AND when the event matches Subject (custom) is not N/A
AND when any of Subject (custom) match (?i)congratulations|\burgent|pharmacy|lottery|payment|purchase|deposit|refund|voice\s*mail|charity|donation|delivery|tax|heritage
RED_list: Event name: Inbound Email with Suspicious Subject Keywords
Event description: Detects when an email is received with a suspicious subject or a subject conveying some sense of urgency.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Sender (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
